title: PCI DSS Log Review Compliance

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-generated from legacy markdown 'Regulations' line during the per-UC migration. Requires SME review to fill in version, clause, mode, and assurance for PCI DSS.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS unknown is enforced — Splunk UC-10.12.7: PCI DSS Log Review Compliance.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-10.12.7' running on sourcetype="linux:audit", firewall, IDS in tag=pci, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security, file integrity / FIM TA
spl:
index=pci (sourcetype="linux:audit" OR sourcetype="pan:traffic") tag=pci
| stats count by sourcetype, host, severity
| join type=left max=1 host [| inputlookup pci_asset_inventory.csv | fields host reviewer last_review]
| where isnull(last_review) OR last_review < relative_time(now(),"-7d@d")
description: Demonstrates ongoing review of security events for cardholder data environments (CDE) per PCI DSS Req. 10–12.
value: Demonstrates ongoing review of security events for cardholder data environments (CDE) per PCI DSS Req. 10–12.
implementation: Tag CDE assets; weekly attested review searches with saved reports and sign-off in GRC tooling.
visualization: Table (assets pending review), Pie chart (events by severity), Status (compliance %).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Demonstrates ongoing review of security events for cardholder data environments (CDE) per DSS Req — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, file integrity / FIM TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="linux:audit"`, firewall, IDS in `tag=pci`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Tag CDE assets; weekly attested review searches with saved reports and sign-off in GRC tooling.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pci (sourcetype="linux:audit" OR sourcetype="pan:traffic") tag=pci
| stats count by sourcetype, host, severity
| join type=left max=1 host [| inputlookup pci_asset_inventory.csv | fields host reviewer last_review]
| where isnull(last_review) OR last_review < relative_time(now(),"-7d@d")
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI DSS Log Review Compliance** — Demonstrates ongoing review of security events for cardholder data environments (CDE) per PCI DSS Req. 10–12.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="linux:audit"`, firewall, IDS in `tag=pci`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security, file integrity / FIM TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pci; **sourcetype**: linux:audit, pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pci, sourcetype="linux:audit", tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sourcetype, host, severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(last_review) OR last_review < relative_time(now(),"-7d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (assets pending review), Pie chart (events by severity), Status (compliance %).
